rationale: Both correctly diagnose the invalid/wrong-run-level root cause and match the thread's actual resolution, both citing run_args.py/stage_config.py for the dummy-load-format mechanism and both proposing the same --run-level=full_model fix. Y goes further by tracing the full path (marker vs run-level distinction, runtime.py's tiny-model swap, assertions.py gating) and by flagging two extra practical gaps (video path must exist locally, A100 vs the H100/MI325 hardware_test declaration) that directly serve the reporter's actual run. X is equally correct but slightly thinner in grounding, though it does supply concrete conftest guard code where Y only describes the guard conceptually.
